# COG Actions Audit — 2026-04-18

**Scope:** catalog every exported function in `lib/actions/cog-records.ts` (643 L) and `lib/actions/mass-upload.ts` (1093 L) so that **cog-data-rewrite subsystem 9** (mega-file split) has a concrete split target before any refactor begins.

**Generated by:** `grep -nE "^export (async )?function|^export (const|async)"` + manual classification.

**Total lines audited:** 1,736 across 2 files.

---

## 1. `lib/actions/cog-records.ts` (643 lines)

### Function catalog

| # | Function | Start line | Role | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | `getCOGRecords(input)` | 20 | Read — paginated list with `COGFilters` | ~80 L; vendor-name backfill + enrichment read |
| 2 | `createCOGRecord(input)` | 101 | Write — single insert | ~29 L |
| 3 | `bulkImportCOGRecords(input)` | 130 | Write — batched insert with per-row dedup + vendor resolve | ~162 L — largest function in file |
| 4 | `getVendorCOGSpend(vendorId)` | 292 | Read — rollup `SUM(extendedPrice)` | ~16 L |
| 5 | `computePricingVsCOG(...)` | 308 | Compute — variance of PricingFile vs COG | ~66 L |
| 6 | `matchCOGToContracts()` | 374 | Enrichment — rematches `COGRecord → Contract` | ~107 L — **core enrichment target** |
| 7 | `deleteCOGRecord(id)` | 481 | Write — single delete | ~7 L |
| 8 | `bulkDeleteCOGRecords(ids)` | 488 | Write — bulk delete | ~10 L |
| 9 | `clearAllCOGRecords()` | 498 | Write — delete-all for facility | ~10 L |
| 10 | `deleteCOGFileByDate(dateStr)` | 508 | Write — delete by transactionDate | ~17 L |
| 11 | `updateCOGRecord(...)` | 525 | Write — single update | ~31 L |
| 12 | `getCOGImportHistory(facilityId?)` | 556 | Read — import history (currently reads `COGRecord.createdAt` grouping; **should migrate to new `FileImport` table** in subsystem 1+) | ~29 L |
| 13 | `getCOGStats(facilityId)` | 585 | Read — summary stats | ~58 L |

### Findings

- **Largest function:** `bulkImportCOGRecords` (~162 L) — batch insert, dedup check, vendor resolution all in one. Subsystem 1 will extract vendor-resolve + dedup into shared helpers.
- **Duplicated vendor-matching:** `bulkImportCOGRecords` has inline vendor resolution; `matchCOGToContracts` has its own. Both should delegate to `lib/vendors/resolve.ts::resolveVendorId` (already exists).
- **Sign-convention compliance:** No violations found — `computePricingVsCOG` returns `contractPrice - pricingPrice` which aligns with "positive savings = facility paid less" (we're comparing contract floor vs pricing-file ceiling, not invoice vs contract).
- **`getCOGImportHistory` is stale:** rolls up by `transactionDate` from `COGRecord.createdAt` as a proxy for "which file each row came from." With the new `FileImport` model, this should read from `FileImport` directly and join `COGRecord` via `fileImportId`. Tracked as subsystem-1 work.

### Split target for subsystem 9

Keep `cog-records.ts` as a root facade ≤300 L; extract into `lib/actions/cog/`:
- `cog/read.ts` — `getCOGRecords`, `getVendorCOGSpend`, `getCOGImportHistory`, `getCOGStats`, `computePricingVsCOG` (~5 functions, ~240 L target)
- `cog/write.ts` — `createCOGRecord`, `updateCOGRecord`, `deleteCOGRecord`, `bulkDeleteCOGRecords`, `clearAllCOGRecords`, `deleteCOGFileByDate` (~6 functions, ~75 L target)
- `cog/import.ts` — `bulkImportCOGRecords` + extracted vendor-resolve + dedup helpers (~200 L target)
- `cog/enrichment.ts` — `matchCOGToContracts` + new `recomputeCOGEnrichment(facilityId, contractId?)` from cog-data subsystem 2 (~180 L target)
- `cog-records.ts` (root) — re-exports + any truly cross-cutting helpers (~50 L target)

---

## 2. `lib/actions/mass-upload.ts` (1093 lines)

### Top-level exports (server actions)

| # | Function | Start | Domain | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | `ingestExtractedContracts(...)` | 234 | **Contracts** | ~131 L — heaviest action; calls rich-extract pipeline, maps AI output to `Contract` + `ContractTerm` + `ContractTier` |
| 2 | `ingestExtractedInvoices(...)` | 366 | **Invoices** | ~150 L |
| 3 | `ingestCaseDataCSV(...)` | 517 | **Case-costing** | ~122 L |
| 4 | `ingestCaseProceduresCSV(...)` | 640 | **Case-costing** | ~101 L |
| 5 | `ingestCaseSuppliesCSV(...)` | 742 | **Case-costing** | ~118 L |
| 6 | `ingestCOGRecordsCSV(...)` | 861 | **COG** | ~79 L — delegates to `bulkImportCOGRecords` |
| 7 | `ingestPricingFile(...)` | 941 | **Pricing** | ~152 L |

### Module-level helpers (non-exported)

| Symbol | Line | Scope | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|
| `TargetField` (type) | 29 | shared | Column mapping contract |
| `mapColumnsWithGemini(...)` | 31 | shared | AI column-mapping (Claude now per ai-rewrite commit); used by all ingest flows |
| `NORM` | 89 | shared | String normalization |
| `localFallbackMap(...)` | 91 | shared | Deterministic fallback for column mapping |
| `get(...)` | 109 | shared | Row-field getter |
| `findOrCreateVendorByName(...)` | 137 | contracts | Should delegate to `lib/vendors/resolve` |
| `toContractType` / `toPerfPeriod` / `toTermType` / `toRebateType` | 156-205 | contracts | AI-schema → Prisma-enum coercion |
| `toSafeDate(...)` | 216 | shared | Date parsing safety |
| `IngestContractInput` / `IngestContractResult` (types) | 224, 230 | contracts | |
| `IngestInvoiceInput` / `IngestInvoiceResult` (types) | 354, 362 | invoices | |
| `parseCSV(text)` | 437 | shared | Header-first CSV parser |
| `parseMoney(raw)` | 480 | shared | Currency-string → number |
| `parseDate(raw)` | 489 | shared | Date string → Date |

### Domain breakdown

| Domain | Exported functions | Approx lines | Split target |
|---|---|---|---|
| COG | 1 (`ingestCOGRecordsCSV`) | 79 | → `lib/actions/imports/cog-import.ts` |
| Pricing | 1 (`ingestPricingFile`) | 152 | → `lib/actions/imports/pricing-import.ts` |
| Contracts | 1 (`ingestExtractedContracts`) + 4 coercion helpers | ~180 | → `lib/actions/imports/contract-import.ts` |
| Invoices | 1 (`ingestExtractedInvoices`) | 150 | → `lib/actions/imports/invoice-import.ts` |
| Case-costing | 3 (`ingestCaseDataCSV`, `ingestCaseProceduresCSV`, `ingestCaseSuppliesCSV`) | 341 | → `lib/actions/imports/case-costing-import.ts` |
| Shared helpers | `mapColumnsWithGemini`, `parseCSV`, `parseMoney`, `parseDate`, `NORM`, `localFallbackMap`, `get`, `toSafeDate`, `TargetField` | ~180 | → `lib/actions/imports/shared.ts` |

### Findings

- **File is a god object:** 7 server actions spanning 5 domains. This is the single highest-leverage split in the cog-data-rewrite spec.
- **AI column-mapping is shared:** `mapColumnsWithGemini` (now claude-backed but name unchanged) is used across all ingest flows — rename to `mapColumnsWithAI` during subsystem 9 refactor.
- **Case-costing is the biggest cluster:** 3 functions ≈ 341 L. Should move together into a dedicated file that imports the shared parsers.
- **`ingestExtractedContracts` has 4 inline enum-coercion helpers** — these belong in `lib/contracts/ai-mapping.ts` (new) or colocated with the contract-import file.
- **No sign-convention violations:** variance/savings math is not computed in this file; upstream `bulkImportCOGRecords` handles it.
- **Vendor resolution duplicated:** `findOrCreateVendorByName` is a second vendor-resolve path that should consolidate into `lib/vendors/resolve.ts`.

### Split target for subsystem 9

```
lib/actions/
├── mass-upload.ts                  (≤150 L — thin orchestrator, re-exports)
└── imports/
    ├── shared.ts                   (~200 L — parseCSV, parseMoney, parseDate, mapColumnsWithAI, NORM, localFallbackMap, get, toSafeDate)
    ├── cog-import.ts               (~90 L)
    ├── pricing-import.ts           (~170 L)
    ├── contract-import.ts          (~200 L — ingestExtractedContracts + enum coercion helpers)
    ├── invoice-import.ts           (~170 L)
    └── case-costing-import.ts      (~360 L — 3 functions)
```

**Total target:** 6 files ≈ 1,190 L, replacing one 1,093 L file. Net +100 L (import overhead) is acceptable; the point is per-domain locality, not fewer total lines.

---

## 3. Recommendation for subsystem 9 execution order

Do the splits in this order (lowest-risk first):

1. **`mass-upload.ts` split** — pure mechanical extraction, 6 new files, no behavior change. Validate via `bunx tsc --noEmit` + existing e2e tests.
2. **`cog-records.ts` split** — similar mechanical extraction into `lib/actions/cog/`. Validate via same commands.
3. **Vendor-resolve consolidation** — collapse `findOrCreateVendorByName` + inline vendor-match in `bulkImportCOGRecords` + `matchCOGToContracts` into `lib/vendors/resolve.ts`. This is a behavior-affecting refactor — needs its own PR.
4. **`getCOGImportHistory` migration to `FileImport`** — this is a subsystem-1 task, not a subsystem-9 split.

---

- `lib/vendors/resolve.ts` — target of vendor-resolve consolidation
- `lib/actions/cog/` — new directory to be created by subsystem 9
- `lib/actions/imports/` — new directory to be created by subsystem 9
- `components/facility/mass-upload/mass-upload.tsx` (1454 L) — has its own tech-debt file-split ticket; not audited here
